title: Why Relationships Matter
body:
Relationships are one of the biggest determinants of wellbeing. Supportive bonds buffer stress, improve recovery from failure, and make people more capable of taking risks in the rest of life.

They also influence practical outcomes: who helps you when things go wrong, who challenges your assumptions, and who becomes part of the decision-making circle around work, health, and family.

Learning relationships well gives people a better map of themselves. Repeating patterns in conflict, avoidance, control, or care often reveal more about a person than their preferences ever will.
keyPoints: Strong relationships improve wellbeing and resilience
Poor relationship patterns often leak into work and health
Relationship literacy helps people choose partners and friends more wisely
Repair skills matter more than being perfectly matched
Healthy boundaries make deeper care possible
furtherReading: The Relationship Cure by John Gottman
Attached by Amir Levine and Rachel Heller
Why We Love by Helen Fisher
